api/socket-events.title: Socket.IO Events
api/socket-events.description: Tonkatsu uses Socket.IO for all real-time communication. Connect to http3001.

architecture/agent-workspace.title: Agent Workspace
architecture/agent-workspace.description: Each agent gets a dedicated directory under workspaces///. This directory is the agent's entire world — it's the working directory for Claude Code, the source of its identity, and the storage for its memory.

architecture/client.title: Client
architecture/client.description: The client is a React 19 + TypeScript application bundled with Vite. All source files are in client/src/.

architecture/overview.title: Overview
architecture/overview.description: This page explains the big picture — how the pieces fit together, what happens when you send a message, and why things are built the way they are. No technical background required for the first half.

architecture/server.title: Server
architecture/server.description: The server is an Express + Socket.IO application written in TypeScript (ESM), running under tsx watch for hot-reload in development. All source files are in server/src/.

deployment.title: Deployment
deployment.description: Running Tonkatsu in production requires building both packages, configuring the server environment, and optionally setting up a reverse proxy and process manager.

examples/first-agent.title: Your First Agent
examples/first-agent.description: This walkthrough creates a coding assistant from scratch and shows you exactly what happens when you send it a task.

examples/multi-agent-team.title: Multi-Agent Team
examples/multi-agent-team.description: This example sets up two assistants that work together: a coordinator that manages the work, and a writer that handles the actual writing. The coordinator delegates — the writer delivers.

examples/repo-backed-agent.title: Repo-Backed Agent
examples/repo-backed-agent.description: A repo-backed agent works directly inside a git repository. Its code changes are tracked in git on a dedicated branch, while its identity files (SOUL.md, MEMORY.md, etc.) stay private and are never committed.

examples/scheduled-tasks.title: Scheduled Tasks
examples/scheduled-tasks.description: Agents can run tasks on a cron schedule — useful for daily standups, monitoring alerts, periodic data syncs, or any recurring automation.

getting-started.title: Getting Started
getting-started.description: This guide walks you through installing Tonkatsu, creating your first AI assistant, and understanding how your data is stored.

security.title: Security
security.description: Tonkatsu is designed for self-hosted, trusted-operator use. This page covers the security model, potential risks, and how to reduce exposure.

troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting
troubleshooting.description: Something not working? This page covers the most common issues and how to fix them.
